Cryozoo overview trees

This script was mostly written by claude to generate an overview style tree plot for the cryozoo. With one tree per class based on the Open tree of life topologies

Biobank Species Overview — Circular Phylogenetic Trees + PhyloPic silhouettes

Dependencies:

install.packages(c(“tidyverse”, “rotl”, “ape”, “rphylopic”,

“patchwork”, “readxl”, “RColorBrewer”))

BiocManager::install(c(“ggtree”, “ggtreeExtra”))

rphylopic >= 1.4.0 required (CRAN): install.packages(“rphylopic”)

library(ggtreeExtra)

Error in library(ggtreeExtra) : there is no package called ‘ggtreeExtra’

0. CONFIGURATION — tweak colours, paths, scaling here

IUCN_COLOURS

       LC        NT        VU        EN        CR        EW        DD        NE 
"#60C659" "#CCE226" "#F9E814" "#FC7F3F" "#D81E05" "#542344" "#D1D1C6" "#AAAAAA"

1. Read table with all cryozoo species

columns: species, class, order, IUCN status

unique(iucn_status$iucn_status)

[1] LC   VU   NT   EN   CR   DD   <NA> EW   NE  
Levels: LC NT VU EN CR EW DD NE

2. PHYLOPIC — fetch and cache silhouettes for qualifying orders

print(order_summary)

# A tibble: 9 × 3
  class    order          n_species
  <chr>    <chr>              <int>
1 Amphibia Anura                 18
2 Aves     Passeriformes         13
3 Aves     Pelecaniformes        11
4 Aves     Psittaciformes        15
5 Mammalia Artiodactyla          38
6 Mammalia Carnivora             24
7 Mammalia Primates              28
8 Reptilia Squamata              32
9 Reptilia Testudines            10
# Fetch one silhouette per qualifying order; cache as named list of raster imgs.
# pick_phylopic() finds the best UUID match; get_phylopic() downloads the raster.
fetch_phylopic_safe <- function(order_name) {
  tryCatch({
  uid <- rphylopic::get_uuid(name = order_name)
    img <- rphylopic::get_phylopic(uuid = uid)
    message("  OK: ", order_name)
    img
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("  WARN: PhyloPic not found for '", order_name, "' — ", e$message)
    NULL
  })
}

message("\nFetching PhyloPic silhouettes...")
phylopic_cache <- order_summary %>%
  pull(order) %>%
  set_names() %>%
  map(fetch_phylopic_safe) %>%
  compact()

message(
  length(phylopic_cache), " / ", nrow(order_summary),
  " silhouettes fetched successfully."
)

3. HELPER — fetch OTL tree for one class


fetch_class_tree <- function(species_vec, class_name) {

  message("  Fetching OTL tree for ", class_name, " (", length(species_vec), " spp)")

  matched <- tryCatch(
    tnrs_match_names(species_vec, context_name = NULL),
    error = function(e) { message("    OTL match failed: ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(matched)) return(NULL)

  ott_ids <- matched %>%
    filter(!is.na(ott_id), !flags %in% c("barren", "extinct")) %>%
    pull(ott_id) %>%
    unique()

  if (length(ott_ids) < 3) {
    message("    Too few resolved names (", length(ott_ids), "), skipping")
    return(NULL)
  }

  tree <- tryCatch(
    tol_induced_subtree(ott_ids = ott_ids, label_format = "name"),
    error = function(e) { message("    Subtree fetch failed: ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(tree)) return(NULL)

  tree$tip.label <- tree$tip.label %>%
    str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
    str_remove("\\s+ott\\d+$")

  tree <- compute.brlen(tree, method = "Grafen")
  tree <- chronos(tree, quiet = TRUE)
  tree
}

4. HELPER — compute angular midpoint for a set of tips in a circular tree

ggtree places n tips at equal angular spacing.

We identify where our order’s tips fall and take the circular mean angle,

then convert to Cartesian (x, y) at the desired placement radius.



tips_to_xy <- function(tree, tip_subset, radius) {

  n          <- length(tree$tip.label)
  tip_angles <- tibble(
    label = tree$tip.label,
    # ggtree default: 90° at top, counter-clockwise, in degrees
    angle_deg = 90 - 360 * (seq_len(n) - 1) / n
  )

  subset_ang <- tip_angles %>%
    filter(label %in% tip_subset) %>%
    pull(angle_deg)

  if (length(subset_ang) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Circular mean (handles ±180° wrap-around correctly)
  rad    <- subset_ang * pi / 180
  mean_x <- mean(cos(rad))
  mean_y <- mean(sin(rad))
  mid_rad <- atan2(mean_y, mean_x)

  tibble(
    x         = radius * cos(mid_rad),
    y         = radius * sin(mid_rad),
    angle_deg = mid_rad * 180 / pi
  )
}

5. HELPER — build one annotated circular ggtree plot with PhyloPics

6. HELPER — shared legend panel

7. plot Taxonomies

trees <- class_counts %>%
  pull(class) %>%
  set_names() %>%
  map(function(cls) {
    sp_vec <- species_list %>%
      filter(!!sym(COL_CLASS) == cls) %>%
      pull(!!sym(COL_SPECIES))
    fetch_class_tree(sp_vec, cls)
  }) %>%
  compact()


Progress [---------------------------------] 0/448 (  0%) ?s
Progress [==============================] 448/448 (100%)  0s
                                                            

Progress [---------------------------------] 0/647 (  0%) ?s
Progress [==============================] 647/647 (100%)  0s
                                                            

Progress [---------------------------------] 0/285 (  0%) ?s
Progress [==============================] 285/285 (100%)  0s
                                                            

Progress [---------------------------------] 0/147 (  0%) ?s
Progress [==============================] 147/147 (100%)  0s
                                                            

Progress [---------------------------------] 0/166 (  0%) ?s
Progress [==============================] 166/166 (100%)  0s
                                                            
class_plots <- trees %>%
  imap(~ build_class_plot(
    tree          = .x,
    meta_df       = species_list,
    class_name    = .y,
    phylopic_imgs = phylopic_cache
  ))

Error in `map2()`:
ℹ In index: 1.
ℹ With name: Mammalia.
Caused by error in `if (stats::var(as.numeric(dat[[xid]]), na.rm = TRUE) != 0) ...`:
! missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed
Run `]8;;x-r-run:rlang::last_trace()rlang::last_trace()]8;;` to see where the error occurred.
# ── 7d. Relative panel sizes (sqrt-scaled, largest first) ─────────────────────
sizes_ordered <- class_counts %>%
  filter(class %in% names(class_plots)) %>%
  mutate(size_weight = SIZE_SCALE_FN(n_species)) %>%
  arrange(desc(size_weight)) %>%
  select(class, size_weight) %>%
  deframe()

Error in `filter()`:
ℹ In argument: `class %in% names(class_plots)`.
Caused by error:
! object 'class_plots' not found
Run `]8;;x-r-run:rlang::last_trace()rlang::last_trace()]8;;` to see where the error occurred.


# ── 7e. Multipanel layout: 2 rows, larger classes on top ──────────────────────
n_plots      <- length(class_plots)
n_top        <- ceiling(n_plots / 2)

top_plots    <- class_plots[seq_len(n_top)]
bottom_plots <- class_plots[seq(n_top + 1, n_plots)]
top_sizes    <- sizes_ordered[seq_len(n_top)]
bottom_sizes <- sizes_ordered[seq(n_top + 1, n_plots)]

top_row    <- wrap_plots(top_plots,    widths = top_sizes,    nrow = 1)
bottom_row <- wrap_plots(bottom_plots, widths = bottom_sizes, nrow = 1)
legend_row <- build_legend()

combined <- (top_row / bottom_row / legend_row) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(
    max(top_sizes),
    max(bottom_sizes),
    max(top_sizes) * 0.18
  )) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Biobank Species Overview",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Annotation rings (inside \u2192 outside): ",
      "IUCN status \u2502 WGS \u2502 RNA-seq \u2502 ATAC-seq \u2502 Reference genome\n",
      "PhyloPic silhouettes shown outside outermost ring ",
      "for orders with \u2265 ", PHYLOPIC_MIN_SPECIES, " species"
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",    hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8.5, colour = "grey40", hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )

combined

# ── 7f. Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggsave("biobank_overview.pdf", combined,
       width = 24, height = 20, units = "in", device = cairo_pdf)

ggsave("biobank_overview.png", combined,
       width = 24, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 300)

message("\nDone! Saved to biobank_overview.pdf / .png")
